summary: List comprehensions construct lists concisely: `[expr for item in sequence if cond]`. Faster than append loops.
oneLineSummary: List comprehension: concise `[expr for item in iter if cond]` syntax for building lists.
keyTakeaways: Ternary goes before `for`, filter goes after `for`.

memoryTricks.trick: [WHAT_TO_KEEP for ITEM in LIST if FILTER]

commonErrors.cause: Placing ternary else clause after for

preInterviewChecklist: Explain list comprehension vs generator expression

nextTopics.title: Dictionaries & Sets
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn key-value mappings and unique sets.
